scenes.text: FieldLens — an AI-powered field inspection platform, built on Amazon Nova and Amazon Bedrock.
Field inspections are broken. Inspectors spend over four hours per visit on manual documentation — paper forms, disconnected photos, and voice notes that never make it into official reports.
FieldLens cuts that to under five minutes. Using Amazon Nova's multimodal reasoning, voice transcription, and semantic embeddings — raw field data becomes a structured AI-generated report, instantly.

The inspector opens the app and enters site details — location, client, and project ID. They capture photo evidence on-site. Nova two Lite automatically analyzes each image, tagging defects and materials in real time. Nova two Sonic transcribes their voice walkthrough — turning spoken observations into structured field notes instantly. Additional notes and annotations complete the inspection record. The inspector reviews the AI-assembled report before submitting. With one tap, the data is sent to Amazon Bedrock — Nova two Lite generates the full inspection report. The result: a complete, structured report with findings, photos, and recommendations — in seconds.

Here's how FieldLens is built. The mobile app routes through a backend AI pipeline powered by Amazon Bedrock. Nova two Lite handles multimodal reasoning. Nova two Sonic transcribes voice. Nova Embeddings power semantic search. The web dashboard delivers real-time visibility — all on AWS: S3, RDS, Cognito, Lambda, and CloudFront.

This isn't a prototype — it's real. We ran forty-seven Nova model invocations across three model types, all through Amazon Bedrock. Every AI-generated report you'll see was produced by live Nova API calls.

The web dashboard shows a real-time inspection feed. New submissions arrive live via WebSocket with instant status updates. The inspections list is fully searchable — filter by site, status, or date range. Nova-powered analytics surfaces defect trends, inspector performance, and compliance tracking — turning field data into actionable insights.
Let's see the full workflow in action — from opening the mobile app on site, through AI-powered report generation, to reviewing the final output on the web dashboard.
FieldLens. Powered by Amazon Nova. Built for the AWS Nova AI Hackathon 2025 — transforming field inspections with Nova two Lite, Nova two Sonic, and Nova Embeddings on Amazon Bedrock.
